methodology: 95.5% preseason projections + 4.5% Week 1 roto-style category performance (1/22 of season played). Each week of real data proportionally replaces projection weight. Right now, the spreadsheet barely flinches.

rankings.blurb: The preseason #1 posted an 8.03 ERA, a 1.70 WHIP, lost to Duran Duran 6-7-3 in categories, and finished 11th in roto. And guess what? They're still #1 in the power rankings. Projections don't care about your feelings. Judge, Soto, Harper, Freeman, and Riley combined for a .225 batting average in Week 1, which is the kind of collective underperformance that should be studied by scientists. The model says this roster is too talented to stay down, and at 95.5% preseason weight, one ugly week is basically a rounding error. If they're still posting 8.03 ERAs in June, we'll talk. Until then, the crown stays.

A 7-5-4 win over Nine Spoons with 22 runs, 7 dingers, and a .478 SLG — the bats showed up with a vendetta. The pitching posted zero wins, zero quality starts, and 11 strikeouts, which is the kind of staff line that makes you want to hide the box score in a drawer. Roto rank of 6th feels harsh given the offensive fireworks, but 4.50 ERA and 1.50 WHIP will do that to you. None of it matters for the power rankings though — at 4.5% actual weight, this team could've gone 16-0-0 and still been #2. Bobby Witt and Lindor are doing Bobby Witt and Lindor things. Status quo.

The team projected 0-11 against the field in preseason just beat The Polar Express 8-5-3. Redemption indeed. 53 strikeouts led the entire league. 5 saves+holds — also league-best. The pitching carried this team up Everest in a blizzard. But the offense — 14 runs, 1 homer, 14 RBI, and a .348 SLG — suggests the hitters are using baguettes instead of bats. Roto 7th, preseason 3rd, blend stays at 3rd. One week changes nothing on the spreadsheet but it changes everything in our hearts. If the bats ever find a pulse, this pitching staff could drag them into contention for real.

Skenes, Ohtani on the mound, Ragans — the pitching was supposed to be the identity — and they delivered a 5.59 ERA with zero wins, zero saves+holds, and 27 strikeouts. That K total is embarrassing for a staff with this pedigree. The bats showed some life with 7 homers and 4 steals, but a .206 AVG means a lot of those at-bats ended in existential despair. The 5-7-4 loss to Young and Yandy stings. Roto says 8th, preseason says 4th, blend says still 4th. The formula is giving them a very long leash. Whether they deserve it depends entirely on whether Skenes remembers he's Paul Skenes.

Wait...wait...wait...wait — this team got demolished 2-12-2 by Señor Burns, posted the second-best roto numbers in the entire league, and didn't move a single spot? Welcome to the blend, where 4.5% of excellence meets 95.5% of preseason shrugging. A 2.36 ERA, 1.01 WHIP, 51 Ks, 8 homers, and a .448 SLG says this team is legit. They just happened to draw the one manager who went full nuclear in Week 1. The 1 stolen base is a problem, and 17 RBI from a lineup hitting 8 homers is unfortunate sequencing. If Caminero keeps performing at a roto #2 pace, the blend will catch up by May. Patience is a virtue. Also a requirement.

Excellent. 27 runs. 10 homers. 27 RBI. A .318 AVG. A .441 OBP. A .568 SLG. A 12-2-2 annihilation of Caminero Crush. First in roto across the league — six first-place finishes on offense alone. And the reward for the single greatest Week 1 performance in CFBL history? Staying exactly at #6. Wait...wait...wait...wait — that can't be right. Oh, but it is. At 4.5% weight, being roto #1 versus preseason #6 moves the needle from 6.00 to 5.77. The spreadsheet barely raised an eyebrow. Patience, grasshopper. If Burns keeps this pace for three more weeks, the blend will start to believe. Until then, the model says "nice week, prove it again."

Lost 6-8-2 to Camp of the New Orleans Saints but posted the best ERA (2.18) and WHIP (0.91) in the entire league. Those are video game numbers. The 4.09 K/BB ratio was second-best too. So why the loss? Because 15 RBI and a .300 OBP from the offense is bringing a butter knife to a steak dinner. Roto says 5th, preseason says 7th, blend says 7th. A two-rank roto improvement barely registers at this weight — it'll take sustained performance to climb. If the bats ever wake up even slightly, those pitching ratios could carry this team into the top 4 over the long haul.

A convincing 10-3-3 beatdown of PO' BOYD SANDWICH, fueled by a league-second .385 OBP and a 3.02 ERA that had no business being that good. Roto says 4th-best team in the league — four spots higher than preseason. But at 4.5% weight, outperforming by four ranks translates to moving from 8.00 to 7.82 on the blend. You could sneeze and miss it. Ohtani, Acuna, Tatis, Elly — the star power is undeniable. Zero saves+holds and only 1 stolen base from a team with Elly De La Cruz is like having a Ferrari and driving it in a school zone. Keep performing at roto #4 and by Week 8 this team could crack the top 5. The math will get there. Eventually.

rankings.team_name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints
rankings.blurb: The best K/BB ratio in the league (4.33), a .295 batting average, 25 runs scored, and an 8-6-2 win over Balls to the Wall. Roto says 3rd-best team in the league — six spots higher than the preseason projection of 9th. That's the biggest gap between roto rank and power rank in the entire league, and the blend barely cares. Moving from 9.00 to 8.73 is like rearranging deck chairs on the Titanic of irrelevance. That 5.70 ERA contradicts the beautiful K/BB, meaning the pitchers are striking guys out and then getting torched in between. If Camp keeps performing at roto #3, by mid-May the blend will start taking them seriously. Six more weeks of proof required.

Dead last in roto with a category sum of 118.5 across 14 categories. A 5.88 ERA, 1.66 WHIP, .279 OBP, and .333 SLG — this team managed to be bad at almost everything simultaneously, which is actually impressive in its own depressing way. The 5-8-3 loss to ShawSwank — a team projected to go 0-11 — is the kind of result that haunts you in the shower. The 3.89 K/BB and 3 saves+holds were the only bright spots, like finding a twenty in a burning building. Preseason said 10th, roto says 12th, blend says 10th. All aboard the struggle bus. The schedule does not get easier.

A 3-10-3 shellacking from CLAUDE 4 COMMISH, and honestly it felt worse than that. The pitching ratios were actually decent — 4.04 ERA and 1.09 WHIP are respectable — but the offense hit .198 with a .305 SLG. That's not hitting, that's flailing at air with mild conviction. 13 runs scored was dead last in the league. Roto 10th is actually better than preseason 11th, so technically the needle moved in the right direction — from 11.00 to 10.95 on the blend. Celebrate accordingly. The roster has arms for days but swings that belong in a museum of things that don't connect.

They beat the preseason #1 team. A 7-6-3 victory over Universal Basic Civale is the kind of upset that makes you believe in miracles, relief pitching, and the concept of sample size. 7 stolen bases led the league, 4 saves+holds tied for best, and 3 quality starts were solid. But a .140 batting average. Point. One. Four. Zero. Roto 9th is three spots better than the preseason 12th, which at full weight would be meaningful — but at 4.5%, it moves the blend from 12.00 to 11.86. A giant-killing upset and the model doesn't even look up from its spreadsheet. Three ranks of roto improvement needs about eight more weeks of proof before the blend starts listening.
